headline: GOLD SURGES To $4,850 As US-Iran Ceasefire Triggers Relief Rally — Silver Rockets 7% To $77, Oil Plunges Below $100
snippet: Gold surged 3% to $4,850 and silver spiked nearly 7% to $77 on April 8 after the US and Iran announced a two-week ceasefire. Oil fell below $100/bbl, weakening the dollar and boosting rate-cut expectations, with gold now consolidating near $4,760.

headline: Founders Metals FIRST DISCOVERY At Antino North — 17.22 g/t Au Over 3.6m In First-Ever Drill Hole, Backed By Gold Fields and B2Gold
snippet: Founders Metals hit 17.22 g/t Au over 3.6m within a 24.6m interval grading 2.81 g/t Au in the first-ever drillhole at Antino North, Suriname — a new discovery 20km from the main Antino camp on a 102,360-ha district-scale land package.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver Drills 4,896 g/t SILVER At Galena — RECORD 64,000m Campaign Launched On 10 New Vein Discoveries
snippet: Americas Gold & Silver (TSX: USA) hit 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m at its Galena Complex in Idaho, revealing 10 new high-grade veins. The company launched its largest-ever 64,000m drill program in 2026, including the newly acquired Crescent Mine.

headline: Pan American Silver La Colorada PEA: 19.1M oz Ag/yr Peak Production, $1.9B Capex, 37-Year Mine Life At Top-Tier Skarn
snippet: Pan American Silver's revised La Colorada PEA projects 19.1M oz Ag/yr at peak from a new 15,000 tpd plant, with $1.9B initial capex and a 37-year mine life. A 6-year construction timeline begins in 2026, with high-grade vein discoveries now folded into the skarn plan.

headline: COPPER Hits $5.87/lb — Third Straight Weekly Gain As JPMorgan Projects 330,000t Supply Shortfall, AI Centers Consume 475,000t
snippet: Copper reached $5.87/lb on April 10, up 29.4% year-over-year, on a third consecutive weekly advance. JPMorgan forecasts a 330,000t refined copper shortfall in 2026 and estimates AI data center demand alone will consume 475,000t — a structural tailwind for copper miners.

headline: Core Lithium RESTARTS Finniss — Mining Begins May 2026, $205M Fully Funded, Glencore Buys Existing Concentrate Stockpile
snippet: Australia's Core Lithium has greenlit Finniss after securing $205M, with open-pit mining at the Grants pit beginning in May 2026 and first spodumene concentrate (SC5) delivery expected Q4. Glencore signed a binding agreement to purchase the existing stockpile from care-and-maintenance.

headline: RIO-GLENCORE $260 Billion Mega-Merger COLLAPSES — Valuation Gap Ends What Would Have Been World's Largest Mining Company
snippet: Rio Tinto and Glencore abandoned talks on a $260 billion combination that would have leapfrogged BHP to create the world's largest miner. Negotiations collapsed over equity split — Glencore sought 40% of the merged entity while Rio Tinto offered roughly 32–33%.

headline: Mining M&A Hits 13-Year HIGH — $93.7B Transacted In 2025, Mega-Deals Up 68%, Gold Remains Most Targeted Commodity
snippet: Global mining M&A hit $93.7 billion in 2025, the highest since 2012, with billion-dollar mega-deals surging 68% from 2024. January 2026 kicked off with 85 transactions totalling over $11B, and gold remains the dominant acquisition target commodity.

headline: Uranium Declared US NATIONAL SECURITY ASSET Under Section 232 — AI Data Centers Turbocharge Nuclear Demand, Spot Tops $100/lb
snippet: A January 2026 Section 232 proclamation classifies reliance on foreign uranium as a national security risk, opening the door to import price floors that could decouple US uranium pricing from global spot. Uranium has already exceeded $100/lb as AI-driven electricity demand accelerates nuclear buildout.

headline: F3 Uranium Extends Athabasca Tetra Zone 125% — 37,700 cps Peak Radioactivity As Winter Program Advances Broach Discovery
snippet: F3 Uranium (TSX-V: FUU) extended the Tetra Zone mineralized plunge from 60m to 135m — a 125% increase — with peak radioactivity of 37,700 cps at the Broach property in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin. A 3,000m winter follow-up program is now drilling to expand the system down-plunge.

headline: Gold Miners STILL At 0.75x NAV While Metal Hits Records — Sprott Calls Sector Underinvested Despite Record AISC Margins
snippet: Senior gold miners trade at 0.75x P/NAV and 8x EV/EBITDA — well below long-term averages — while AISC margins hit $19.7/oz in H1 2025, the highest in over a decade. Sprott argues institutional underweight in the sector creates a compelling contrarian entry at current levels.

headline: Hecla Mining DOUBLES Exploration To $55M — 231M oz Silver Reserves, Keno Hill Bermingham Vein Extends 140 Feet Beyond Resource
snippet: Hecla Mining is nearly doubling exploration to $55M in 2026 after reporting 231M oz silver reserves and record 5.3M oz production at Lucky Friday. Keno Hill's Bermingham Vein extended 140 feet beyond the prior resource boundary, returning 36.4 oz/t Ag, 3.4% Zn and 3.4% Pb over 21.4 feet.

headline: CHINA Imports 790+ Tonnes Silver In Early 2026 — Solar Manufacturing Surge Helps Metal OUTPERFORM Gold By 150% Over Past Year
snippet: China's solar manufacturing boom drove 790+ tonnes of silver imports in early 2026, helping the metal post a 150% gain over the past year — far outpacing gold. The gold/silver ratio compression points to structural industrial demand that could sustain silver's outperformance into H2.

headline: Troilus Mining Launches 40,000m Quebec Drill Campaign — Z87 Underground Targets, Bear Lake Regional Tests As Feasibility Nears
snippet: Troilus Mining (TSX: TLG) outlined a 40,000m drill campaign for 2026 targeting near-mine resource growth and high-grade zone definition at its 435 km² Quebec property. Approximately 25,000m targets the Z87 underground corridor, designed to tighten the resource ahead of a construction decision.

headline: Analysts Target $6,900–$7,000 Gold As Metal Consolidates Near $4,760 — Central Banks Still Buying At Elevated Pace
snippet: Multiple analysts are projecting gold at $6,900–$7,000 in the current cycle as the metal consolidates near $4,760 after its ceasefire-driven spike to $4,850. The bullish case rests on persistent central bank buying, structural dollar weakness, and institutional portfolios that remain underweight gold.
